---
title: "Basic Dashboard"
format: dashboard
orientation: columns
jupyter: dataxplore-venv    # pin this env; not the default one
---

## Row 1 {height="40%"}

### Column 1

```{python}
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import plotly.express as px

# Sample data
df = pd.DataFrame({
    'x': np.arange(10),
    'y': np.random.randn(10).cumsum()
})

fig = px.line(df, x='x', y='y', title='Line Chart')
fig.show()
```

### Column 2

```{python}
# Bar chart sample
categories = ['A', 'B', 'C', 'D', 'E']
values = [10, 24, 36, 18, 22]

fig = px.bar(
    x=categories,
    y=values,
    title='Bar Chart',
    labels={'x': 'Category', 'y': 'Value'}
)
fig.show()
```

## Row 2 {height="30%"}

### Column 1

```{python}
# Scatter plot sample
data = pd.DataFrame({
    'x': np.random.randn(100),
    'y': np.random.randn(100),
    'category': np.random.choice(['Group A', 'Group B', 'Group C'], 100)
})

fig = px.scatter(
    data,
    x='x',
    y='y',
    color='category',
    title='Scatter Plot'
)
fig.show()
```

### Column 2

```{python}
# Summary statistics
total = len(df)
mean_value = df['y'].mean()
max_value = df['y'].max()

stats = f"""
- **Total Records**: {total}
- **Mean Value**: {mean_value:.2f}
- **Max Value**: {max_value:.2f}
"""

print(stats)
```

## Row 3 {height="30%"}

### Column

```{python}
# Histogram
fig = px.histogram(
    data,
    x='x',
    title='Distribution of Values',
    nbins=20
)
fig.show()
```
